description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves reveal a dark, possibly hollow or deeply shadowed interior, contrasting with the lighter, speckled stone surface. The block's sides are solid and unadorned, with a consistent dark coloration, suggesting a dense and robust construction. The top surface appears to be made of a composite or aggregate stone material, with visible flecks of various earth tones. The grooves are deep and regular, likely designed to provide traction or drainage. This type of block is commonly used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, plazas, or as part of a traffic control system.
description: A rectangular stone block with deep, evenly spaced grooves on its speckled stone top, contrasting with solid dark sides. The grooved surface suggests use as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striped grooves running horizontally across the speckled stone top, with solid dark sides visible.
description_view_1: Side view where the grooves run vertically, emphasizing the depth and regularity of the channels.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with horizontal grooves and the same speckled stone pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with vertical grooves, mirroring the features of view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no moss, chips, or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
